title: React Hook State Update Bug Fixing

statement.markdown: Given a functional component that uses the useState hook to manage a counter state. The component should update the counter state when a button is clicked. However, the current implementation has a bug that prevents the state from being updated correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The function should take an input object with a single property 'initialCounter' representing the initial counter value.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an object with two properties: 'render' (the rendered JSX) and 'counter' (the current counter value).
statement.notes: The function should update the counter state correctly when the button is clicked.

editorial.title: Editorial: Bug Fixing
editorial.intuition: The bug is likely related to the useState hook usage.
editorial.bruteForce: Try updating the state directly without using the useState hook.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the useState hook correctly to update the state.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The code should update the state correctly when the button is clicked.
commonMistakes: Not using the useState hook correctly

starterCode.javascript: function Counter({initialCounter}) { let [counter, setCounter] = useState(0); const handleButtonClick = () => { setCounter(counter + 1); render(); }; return (div>button onClick={handleButtonClick}>Click me/div>); }

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function Counter({initialCounter}) { let [counter, setCounter] = useState(0); const handleButtonClick = () => { counter++; render(); }; return (div>button onClick={handleButtonClick}>Click me/div>); }
